title: JARVIS Step 30 — Answer-Only /api/jarvis/ask Handler (minimal, read-only)
purpose: Implement the smallest safe POST /api/jarvis/ask endpoint that satisfies the Step 29 contract. It is answer-only, read-only, non-mutating, and delegates all safety classification to jarvis_conversation_safety.classify_jarvis_question. The UI is NOT wired to it.
relation_to_steps_20_29: Step 20 polished the UI; 21 live smoke; 22 runbook; 23 known limits & roadmap; 24 panel glossary; 25 conversation layer plan; 26 display-only conversation shell; 27 answer-only backend safety plan; 28 pure local safety classifier; 29 tests-first ask contract (skipped until the route existed). Step 30 implements the answer-only handler so the Step 29 contract tests activate and pass.
files_changed: app.py — added POST /api/jarvis/ask handler and the _jarvis_ask_answer helper, inside the existing JARVIS block (additive).
tests/test_jarvis_ask_contract.py — retired the three Step 29 'endpoint absent' assertions now that the handler exists; the @requires_ask contract tests now activate unchanged.
tests/test_jarvis_route.py — repointed the obsolete Step 26 ask-absence guard to refresh-only (still asserts /api/jarvis/refresh is absent; all UI safety assertions unchanged).

tests/test_jarvis_conversation_safety.py — repointed the obsolete Step 28 ask-absence guard to refresh-only (renamed to test_step_28_adds_no_refresh_or_execution_endpoint; all classifier assertions unchanged).
explicitly_not_done: No UI wiring of the conversation shell to the endpoint.
No script execution / subprocess / os.system / shell / git call.

endpoint.accepts: JSON object with ONLY a 'question' string.
endpoint.blank_question: Delegated to the classifier -> UNSUPPORTED, returned as 200 with refused=true.
endpoint.safe_behavior: refused=false; conservative read-only answer assembled from already-known JARVIS concepts; sources_used names the read-only sources.
endpoint.forbidden_behavior: refused=true; safety_class begins with FORBIDDEN; short refusal_reason from the classifier; sources_used=[].
endpoint.get: Not registered; FastAPI returns 405 Method Not Allowed.
endpoint.delegation: All safety classification is done by classify_jarvis_question(question); the handler adds only request-shape validation and conservative answer text.

answer_behavior.conservatism: Answers are static/conservative concepts; the handler does not invent live status numbers.

non_execution_guarantees: No subprocess / os.system / shell / git / broker / trading import in the handler.
No file write and no chat-log write anywhere in the request path.
broker_control / paper_ready / live_ready remain false (unchanged by the handler).

repointed_to_refresh_only_in_step_30: test_jarvis_route.py::test_jarvis_step_26_adds_no_ask_or_refresh_endpoint — drops the obsolete '/api/jarvis/ask absent' check; still asserts '/api/jarvis/refresh' absent; UI safety assertions untouched.

test_jarvis_conversation_safety.py::test_step_28_adds_no_ask_endpoint -> renamed test_step_28_adds_no_refresh_or_execution_endpoint — drops the obsolete '/api/jarvis/ask absent' check; still asserts '/api/jarvis/refresh' absent.
tests.note: Step 30 adds an answer-only ask endpoint by design, so the Step 26/28 ask-absence guards became factually obsolete. They were repointed (not deleted) to keep enforcing the still-valid refresh/execution-absence property. The route string is NOT hidden; no contract or forbidden-request test was weakened.

validation.result: PASS (no skips remaining for the ask contract)

recommended_next_safe_step: JARVIS-ASK-UI-WIRING (Step 31, separate approval) — only after this answer-only handler is committed and green, wire the existing display-only conversation shell to POST /api/jarvis/ask with a strictly read-only fetch (no execution controls, no refresh, no chat-log writes), gated by its own UI safety tests. Until then the shell stays display-only and the endpoint is backend-only.
conclusion: A minimal answer-only POST /api/jarvis/ask handler was added that delegates safety to the pure classifier, returns read-only answers or refusals, writes nothing, mutates nothing, and triggers no refresh or trading. The Step 29 contract tests now activate and pass. templates/jarvis.html is unchanged, no UI wiring was added, no chat logs were written, and /api/jarvis/status shape is preserved.
